goal: Land a self-contained criterion benchmark that measures p95 warm LanceDB+IVF/HNSW vector-search latency on a synthetic 768-d corpus and asserts < 100 ms per master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1789.
plan_summary: Add `lancedb`, `arrow-array`, `arrow-schema` as `[dev-dependencies]` to `crates/ucil-embeddings`, register a `[[bench]] name = "vector_query" harness = false` table, write `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs` (synthetic 2000-row corpus, IVF/HNSW index, deterministic seed), `scripts/bench-vector-query.sh` (parses `target/criterion/.../sample.json`, computes per-sample p95, dual-asserts `p95_warm_ms < 100` AND wall-time floor `>= 100_000 ns/iter`), and `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F07.sh` (INDEPENDENTLY re-asserts `p95_warm_ms < 100` from bench-script stdout per WO-0061 mutation-#3 precedent). Pre-baked mutation contract delegates M1/M2/M3 to verifier per WO-0061 line 690.
scope_in: 1. Edit `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml`: add `[dev-dependencies]` entries `lancedb = { workspace = true }`, `arrow-array = { workspace = true }`, `arrow-schema = { workspace = true }` (all three are already workspace deps — Cargo.lock churn expected to be near-zero since `ucil-daemon` already pulls these crates transitively).

2. Edit `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml`: append a second `[[bench]]` table `name = "vector_query"` + `harness = false` AFTER the existing `[[bench]] name = "throughput"` block. Mirror WO-0061 line 685 (frozen `[[bench]]` shape).

3. Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs`. Top-of-file rustdoc cites master-plan §4.2 line 303 + §12.2 lines 1321-1346 + §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1789 + DEC-0016 (lancedb-per-branch closure) + WO-0061 line 685 (canonical bench shape).

4. Bench module exposes a `pub const CORPUS_SIZE: usize = 2000;` and `pub const QUERY_COUNT: usize = 20;` and `pub const EMBEDDING_DIM: usize = 768;` — all three are load-bearing identifiers the WO-0061 mutation pattern stashes. Document the load-bearing-identifier contract inline.

5. Bench setup function (one-time, executed before criterion's measurement loop): (a) `tempfile::tempdir()` for an isolated LanceDB connection root, (b) open `lancedb::connect(path).execute().await`, (c) build a 2-column Arrow schema (`id: Utf8`, `embedding: FixedSizeList<Float32, 768>`) — simplified relative to the production 12-column `code_chunks_schema()` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs:318` per the bench's isolation contract (only the embedding column drives ANN-index query latency), (d) populate `CORPUS_SIZE` rows with deterministic-seed (`StdRng::seed_from_u64(0x_C0DE_BABE)`) random 768-d Float32 vectors, (e) `Table::create_index(&["embedding"], lancedb::index::Index::IvfHnswPq(...))` with `num_partitions = ceil(sqrt(CORPUS_SIZE))` and HNSW defaults — pin literal parameters in the bench source so the verifier can reproduce.

6. Bench query corpus: pre-compute `QUERY_COUNT` distinct 768-d Float32 vectors from a SECOND deterministic-seed RNG (`StdRng::seed_from_u64(0x_FACE_FEED)`) so query data is decorrelated from corpus data. Stored in a `Vec<Vec<f32>>` initialised at setup.

7. Bench function literal name: `vector_query_p95_warm`. Frozen via `group.bench_function("vector_query_p95_warm", |b| b.iter(|| { ... }))` — load-bearing per WO-0061 line 681 because `target/criterion/vector_query_p95_warm/vector_query_p95_warm/new/sample.json` is the parser-script's read path AND `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F07.sh` greps for the literal at the call site.

8. Inside `b.iter`, take a query vector via round-robin index `i % QUERY_COUNT`, call `table.query().nearest_to(query_vec).unwrap().limit(10).execute().await.unwrap()` (`.expect()` permitted in benches per WO-0061 line 689), `black_box` the resulting `RecordBatchStream` to prevent dead-code-elim.

9. Criterion config: `criterion_group!{name = benches; config = Criterion::default().sample_size(100).warm_up_time(Duration::from_secs(2)).measurement_time(Duration::from_secs(20)); targets = bench_vector_query_p95}`. Sample-size 100 is load-bearing — `scripts/bench-vector-query.sh` expects ≥50 samples in `sample.json` to compute a meaningful p95.
10. `criterion_main!(benches);` entry-point.

11. Create NEW script `scripts/bench-vector-query.sh` (executable, `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, `IFS=$'\n\t'`). Top-of-file rustdoc cites master-plan §18 line 1789 + WO-0061 `bench-embed-throughput.sh` precedent.

12. `bench-vector-query.sh` behaviour: (a) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`, (b) NO `install-coderankembed.sh` pre-flight — synthetic corpus does NOT need the real model artefacts (the bench measures LanceDB+ANN-index query latency in isolation; query embeddings are synthetic random vectors), (c) build cache warm via `cargo build --release -p ucil-embeddings --bench vector_query`, (d) run `cargo bench -p ucil-embeddings --bench vector_query`, (e) read `target/criterion/vector_query_p95_warm/vector_query_p95_warm/new/sample.json` (criterion's nested `<group>/<bench>/new/sample.json` shape), (f) jq-extract `times` and `iters` arrays, (g) compute per-sample `mean_ns_per_iter[i] = times[i] / iters[i]`, (h) sort ascending, take element at index `floor(0.95 * len)` for p95, (i) print `p95_warm_ms=<N>` to stdout (one line, exact prefix — verify script greps for this line).

13. `bench-vector-query.sh` two assertion floors (mirror WO-0061 line 679 dual-asserter pattern): (a) WALL-TIME FLOOR — assert `MEAN_NS_PER_ITER >= 100_000` (100 µs per iteration; below this is implausibly fast for a real LanceDB+HNSW query and indicates a stubbed/no-op nearest_to body), fail with `[FAIL] wall-time floor breached` on stderr; (b) p95 FLOOR — assert `p95_warm_ms < 100` per master-plan §18 line 1789, fail with `[FAIL] p95 floor breached` on stderr.

14. Create NEW script `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F07.sh` (executable). Mirrors `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F06.sh` shape: (a) frozen-script existence + executability check on `scripts/bench-vector-query.sh` AND `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs`, (b) frozen-bench-name selector grep `^[[:space:]]*group\.bench_function\("vector_query_p95_warm"` at the criterion call site, (c) run `bash scripts/bench-vector-query.sh` and capture stdout, (d) INDEPENDENTLY parse `^p95_warm_ms=<N>$` from the captured stdout and assert `<N> < 100` (this is the AUTHORITATIVE asserter — bench script's check could be neutered per WO-0061 mutation #3), (e) print `[OK] P2-W8-F07 p95_warm_ms=<N>` and exit 0 on success.
15. Verify script exit semantics: `[FAIL] P2-W8-F07: <reason>` to stderr + exit 1 on any failure. Tail the bench-script's stdout on failure for triage context.

16. Pre-baked mutation contract documented in the WO `acceptance` block AND the executor's `ready-for-review.md` Mutation-execution-log section. Three mutations, each delegated to verifier per WO-0061 line 690 (DO NOT commit-then-revert in-line):

- M1 — `Table::query().nearest_to(...).execute()` body neutered: replace the per-iter call with `let _ = black_box(query_vec);` (drops the actual LanceDB call). Expected failure: wall-time floor breach (`mean_ns_per_iter < 100_000`) AND/OR `cargo bench` panicking on `unwrap()` of an absent stream. Verifier restoration: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs`.

- M2 — `QUERY_COUNT` constant shrunk to 1: edit `pub const QUERY_COUNT: usize = 20;` → `... = 1;`. Expected: round-robin always picks index 0; LanceDB caches the result; per-iter time drops below the 100 µs wall-time floor. Verifier restoration: `git checkout --`.

- M3 — bench script's `< 100` neutered to `< 1000000`: edit `scripts/bench-vector-query.sh` p95-floor branch. Expected: bench script passes (false green); verify script `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F07.sh` INDEPENDENTLY re-asserts `< 100` and FAILS. Verifier restoration: `git checkout --`.

17. Pre-flight word-bans (per WO-0061 line 686): NO literal `mock`, `fake`, `stub`, `fixture` substrings (case-insensitive) in `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs`. Pre-commit grep: `rg -i 'mock|fake|stub|fixture' crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs && exit 1`. The shell scripts MAY use the words (per WO-0061 line 692 — AC mock-scans apply to `.rs` files only).

18. Commit-cadence soft target: 4 commits — `build:` (Cargo.toml dev-deps + bench table), `feat(embeddings):` (vector_query.rs bench file), `chore(scripts):` (bench-vector-query.sh + verify/P2-W8-F07.sh), `docs(work-orders):` (`0065-ready-for-review.md`). Single-commit > 200 LOC for the bench source IS expected per WO-0061 line 697 (large literal arrays + criterion boilerplate); cite DEC-0005 + WO-0061 precedent in the commit body if exceeded.
19. NO new public symbols on `ucil-embeddings` API surface. Bench-only WO. `lib.rs` re-exports unchanged. AC39 cargo-doc gate is N/A (bench files do not appear in `cargo doc` output).

20. Coverage gate is INFORMATIONAL ONLY for this WO (per WO-0061 line 688 — bench-only WO; benches/ is a separate compilation unit outside `cargo llvm-cov` crate-coverage scope, so adding a bench cannot regress coverage). Verifier still runs `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-embeddings --summary-only --json` post-merge to confirm no regression on existing src/ coverage (expect ~89% line — same as pre-WO).
21. Upstream-API research checklist for `lancedb 0.16` (per WO-0060 line 644):
- (a) Does `lancedb::connect(...).execute()` return `Result` or `Connection`? → returns `lancedb::Result<Connection>` (await + `?` chain).
- (b) Is `Connection::create_empty_table(name, schema)` the canonical create entry-point? → YES (used in `branch_manager.rs:452`).
- (c) `Table::add(batches)` vs `Table::add_columns(...)` — which one for bulk insert? → `Table::add(RecordBatchIterator)` per `branch_manager.rs` precedent.

- (d) `Table::create_index(columns, index)` API — what `lancedb::index::Index` variants exist for IVF/HNSW? → `Index::IvfHnswPq` (in `lancedb 0.16`, with `num_partitions`, `num_sub_vectors`, `m`, `ef_construction`, etc. on the builder). Document the picked literal config inline.

- (e) `Table::query().nearest_to(vec)` API — does it return `Result` or builder? → returns a `lancedb::query::Query` builder via `nearest_to` (with `.unwrap()` per `nearest_to`'s `Result`). `.limit(10).execute()` returns a stream.

- (f) Async runtime: `lancedb` futures require `tokio::runtime::Runtime` because criterion's bench loop is sync. Bench setup must wrap async setup in `Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async { ... })` and the per-iter loop must use the same runtime via `runtime.block_on(...)`. Document inline.
scope_out: P2-W8-F08 `find_similar` MCP tool — separate WO (next planner cycle); F08 consumes F07's ANN-index pattern but lives in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` and depends on F08-specific scope (MCP request/response shape, JSON-RPC handler, `BranchManager` accessor wiring).

Production wiring of ANN-index creation in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lancedb_indexer.rs::index_paths` — the lancedb-indexer rustdoc at line 66 explicitly defers ANN-index creation to F07 'consumer side' (the bench creates its own index). Production ANN-index creation hooks into either F08 or a follow-up daemon-side WO.

Real CodeRankEmbed embeddings in the bench corpus — synthetic random 768-d vectors are sufficient to characterise LanceDB+IVF/HNSW query latency in isolation. CodeRankEmbed.embed throughput is benched separately in F06 (WO-0061).

Recall@10 benchmark — out of scope. Master-plan §18 line 1789 lists three benchmarks (throughput, query latency, recall@10); F07 covers query latency only. Recall@10 needs a labelled query/answer corpus (called out as out-of-scope in WO-0061 lessons line 678).
Daemon plumbing of `EmbeddingBackend` / `VectorStoreConfig` (carry from WO-0062 line 747) — orthogonal to F07 bench-only contract.
Production wiring of 4 `G1Source` impls + tracing on `execute_g1` / `fuse_g1` — deferred to P2-W7-F05 per WO-0047/48/51/55/56/58/59/60/61 standing scope_out.
Phase 2 Week 6 close-out doc-rot — deferred to a dedicated docs WO (carry from WO-0045..0062 standing).
Switching `LancedbChunkIndexer` from `Table::add` (append-only) to `merge_insert` upsert semantics — deferred to a future WO per `lancedb_indexer.rs:52-60` rustdoc.

Resolving the `ndarray 0.16` workspace dep vs `ort`-internal `ndarray 0.17` duplication — F07 does not write embedding vectors via `ndarray` (the bench uses `Vec<f32>` + `arrow_array::FixedSizeListArray` builder directly). Defer to a future ndarray-version-bump WO.
Updating any `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` codification of the bench-`.expect()` carve-out — orthogonal docs-WO per WO-0061 line 701.

rejected_alternatives.option: Bundle F07 + F08 into a single WO
rejected_alternatives.reason: F08 is a full MCP-tool implementation (~500 LOC daemon-side: server.rs handler + new `find_similar` module + JSON-RPC request/response shape + integration test). Bundling with F07 (bench-only ~200 LOC) would create a 700+ LOC blast radius spanning two crates (ucil-embeddings benches/ + ucil-daemon src/ + tests/), violate single-WO single-feature precedent set by WO-0058..0062 (each one P2-W8 feature per WO), and create a single-commit-cascade risk on the executor side. Splitting per WO-0061 precedent (one feature per WO, F06-only) is the canonical pattern.

feature-list.json freezes `crate: "ucil-embeddings"` for P2-W8-F07. Moving to ucil-daemon would violate the immutability of the `crate` field (per `ucil-build/CLAUDE.md` whitelist — `crate` is NOT in the six mutable fields). The simplified 2-column bench schema is justified inline relative to the production 12-column `code_chunks_schema()` because only the embedding column drives ANN-index query latency. ucil-embeddings already imports `arrow_array` transitively (via tokenizers? no — directly via this WO's dev-dep). Cycle is avoided: ucil-embeddings does NOT import ucil-daemon.

rejected_alternatives.option: Bench against the real rust-project fixture content with real CodeRankEmbed embeddings
rejected_alternatives.reason: Per master-plan §18 line 1789, F07's metric is 'query latency' — a property of the LanceDB+ANN-index path, not the embedding model. CodeRankEmbed throughput is benched separately in F06. Synthetic 768-d random vectors with a deterministic seed give reproducible measurements at ~3-orders-of-magnitude lower bench wall-time (no 137M-param model load + tokenization).

rejected_alternatives.option: Skip ANN-index creation; rely on LanceDB brute-force kNN
rejected_alternatives.reason: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lancedb_indexer.rs:66-68` rustdoc explicitly contracts F07 to introduce IVF/HNSW index creation: 'No ANN index creation. The IVF/HNSW index for fast vector search is created in P2-W8-F07 (vector query latency benchmark) and consumed in P2-W8-F08 (find_similar MCP tool).' Brute-force kNN at N=2000 would already achieve p95 < 100ms per master-plan §12.2 line 1341 ('1M 128-dim vectors in 33ms'), but the production path needs the ANN index for ≥100K-row branches. Pinning the ANN-index creation in this WO satisfies the daemon-side contract AND exercises the path F08 will consume.

risk_register.risk: `lancedb 0.16` IVF/HNSW index creation is slow on a 2000-row corpus and dominates the bench's wall-time, causing the bench script's measurement to time out.
risk_register.mitigation: ANN-index creation happens ONCE in setup, BEFORE criterion's measurement loop. Criterion's `bench_function` body excludes setup. If setup wall-time exceeds 60s, executor reduces `CORPUS_SIZE` to 1000 (still valid for query-latency characterisation) and re-runs.
risk_register.fallback: If `Index::IvfHnswPq` is not stable in `lancedb 0.16`, fall back to `Index::IvfPq` or brute-force (no index) and document inline. Brute-force at N=2000 should still hit < 100ms p95.

risk_register.risk: `Cargo.lock` churn from `lancedb` dev-dep activation surprises critic.
risk_register.mitigation: Pre-flight diff: `lancedb`, `arrow-array`, `arrow-schema` are ALREADY in `Cargo.lock` (transitively via ucil-daemon since DEC-0016 merge of branch_manager.rs). Adding them as dev-deps to ucil-embeddings should produce a NEAR-ZERO-line diff in `Cargo.lock`. Mention this in the ready-for-review note.

risk_register.risk: Criterion's `sample.json` schema diverges between criterion versions.
risk_register.mitigation: Pin criterion to workspace version (already at `0.5` per existing throughput bench). Bench script's jq parsing tolerates additional fields (extracts only `times` and `iters` arrays via `.times` / `.iters`). Document in script comment.

risk_register.risk: p95 < 100ms target is too lax (LanceDB+HNSW at N=2000 may deliver p95 ~1ms, hiding regressions).
risk_register.mitigation: The 100ms target is master-plan-frozen (§18 line 1789 implicit; §15.x query-duration histogram budget). If the bench delivers < 1ms consistently, executor adds an INFORMATIONAL warning in script stderr: `[INFO] p95_warm_ms=<N> well under 100ms target — consider tightening the floor in a future ADR-driven update`. No AC change.

risk_register.risk: Verifier's clean-slate `cargo build` re-resolves Cargo.lock and deletes the lancedb dev-dep transitive resolution.
risk_register.mitigation: Cargo's lock-file resolution is deterministic given identical Cargo.toml. The verifier's `cargo build --release -p ucil-embeddings --bench vector_query` will re-resolve identically. If Cargo.lock drifts, the verifier should commit the regenerated lock file as part of the verification flow per WO-0058 line 569 precedent.

required_sections: Summary (one paragraph: what landed, which feature flipped)
Acceptance criteria (table: AC# / status / evidence-line / commit-sha)
Mutation execution log (M1/M2/M3: edit applied, expected failure mode, actual stderr line, restoration command + sha after restore)
Cargo.lock diff summary (lines added/removed; expected near-zero)
Bench wall-time breakdown (build cache warm + cargo bench measurement_time + p95 parse)
p95_warm_ms reading (final number; cite the bench-script stdout line)
Coverage gate output (env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-embeddings --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent')
Disclosed deviations (if any inline upstream-API-shape adaptations were needed; cite the lancedb 0.16 source line)
Cited precedents (DEC numbers + WO line numbers)
